ocr.text: In the Fiscal Year 2024 report for Union Pacific in the Transportation & Logistics sector, the Fleet fuel efficiency metrics reveal
significant data across several locomotives, denoted as Locomotive A and Locomotive B, alongside summarized averages.
Locomotive A showcases an Efficiency Rating of 85%, with a Fuel Consumption cost of $1.20 per liter over a Total Distance of
150,000 km. Conversely, Locomotive B exhibits a higher Efficiency Rating of 90%, with a more economical Fuel Consumption
cost of $1.15 per liter while covering a Total Distance of 200,000 km.
The average Efficiency Rating for the fleet, combining both locomotives, stands at 87.5%, reflecting a balanced performance
across operations. The average Fuel Consumption cost is calculated at $1.17 per liter, alongside an aggregated Total Distance
traveled of 175,000 km. This collective assessment leads to an Annual Savings calculation, which totals $500,000 CAD,
indicating effective cost management strategies in fuel utilization. The data clearly illustrate the operational efficiency of Union
Pacific’s locomotive fleet, emphasizing the importance of continuous monitoring and optimization in fuel consumption
practices to enhance overall financial performance.
